RegExp: Performance test. Tests for a specific optimization made by V8's Irregexp, in which patterns of the form (ab|ac|z|ad|ae|af) are optimized into patterns of the form (a[b-f]|z). Due to the size of the pattern involved, TRegex bails out during parsing (https://bugs.chromium.org/p/chromium/issues/detail?id=482998).

Tests an implementation-specific limit in V8.

This v8 test seems to be wrong. It's motivated by an out-of-bounds memory access bug caused by TypedArray.of and TypedArray.from factory methods. In v8's implementation, these factory methods assumed that the newly constructed TypedArray was backed by a live ArrayBuffer. However, changing the constructor to a function that returns an existing TypedArray and then having one of the inputs detach the ArrayBuffer backing that TypedArray as a side-effect of its valueOf method, led v8 to write to invalid memory. As this was a security issue, this was promptly fixed by adding an isDetached check and throwing a TypeError if the ArrayBuffer backing the TypedArray is detached at the time of writing the value. This fixes the out-of-bounds memory access security issue, but it does not align with ECMAScript semantics, since the spec mandates that setting an element of a TypedArray that's backed by a detached ArrayBuffer is just a no-op (https://tc39.es/ecma262/multipage/ordinary-and-exotic-objects-behaviours.html#sec-typedarraysetelement).
Uses the Type Reflection proposal for WebAssembly JS API (via the ToPromising function).

Memory instance size exceeded beyond 4GB. In the current spec, the JS WebAssembly API should not allow such large memory instances. Furthermore, GraalJS is limited to 2GB because of using signed 32-bit integers for indices.
Tests implementation-specific range limits on BigInts.

Fails with LinkError instead of RuntimeError. The failure occurs in module instantiation and the spec for the WebAssembly JS interface states that errors from instantiation are to be signalled using LinkError (except for when the error originates from the start function, which should use RuntimeError). In this case, the error does not originate from the start function, but from the memory initialization using an active data segment. Apart from this and one different error message, we pass all tests in the file.
